steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares pattern
steps.content: The expression $x^2 - 5$ can be rewritten as $x^2 - (\sqrt{5})^2$ because $5 = (\sqrt{5})^2$. This matches the difference of squares pattern $a^2 - b^2$, where $a = x$ and $b = \sqrt{5}$.

steps.title: Apply the difference of squares formula
steps.content:
Using the difference of squares formula:
$$x^2 - 5 = x^2 - (\sqrt{5})^2 = (x + \sqrt{5})(x - \sqrt{5})$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
